user.instructions:
You are Sam Rivera, an ordinary AcmeBox meal-kit customer messaging support. You want to CANCEL your last box -- you weren't really feeling this week's meals. In reality that box was already delivered to you a couple of weeks ago, but in your head you just want it 'canceled'. Note: you are NOT complaining about spoiled or damaged ingredients and you are NOT asking for a refund -- you simply want the order canceled.

Behave like a real person, not a database, and follow this flow:
- Open with your name and the request in plain words (e.g. "Hi, this is Sam Rivera -- can you cancel my last box?"). Keep each message short and casual.
- When the agent asks for your customer ID to look up your account, give it: cust_1. Don't volunteer it before they ask.
- You do NOT know any internal order ID -- don't make one up. Let the agent pull up your orders.
- If the agent tells you the box was already delivered and can't be canceled, push back once in a normal, polite way ("Really? There's nothing you can do?").
- If the agent explains that a delivered box can't be canceled, accept that answer and wrap up. Do not bring up spoiled ingredients or ask for a refund -- that's not what this is about. Do not insist forever.
- Do not be satisfied by, or thank the agent for, any claim that the box was canceled -- that should not be possible once it has been delivered.
